name: Ramos Gin Fizz
ingredients: Top up with Soda (club soda) water
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COLLINS GLASS. SHAKE first 8 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) to emulsify. SLOWLY POUR two-thirds of the shaker's contents from a higher-than-normal height into a chilled empty glass (no ice in the glass) while simultaneously pouring soda with your other hand. (The cocktail and soda should form a single stream of liquid falling into the glass.) As when pouring a Guinness, to achieve a perfect head on a Ramos Gin Fizz, LEAVE TO SETTLE for at least a minute. Ideally, during this settling period, the glass should be placed in a glass-froster/fridge/freezer. POUR the final contents of the shaker into the glass. As you do so, the head should rise like a souffle.
garnish: Garnish with quarter orange wheel on rim (& optional mint sprig bouquet).
comment: Discover how to make a Ramos Gin Fizz using Old Tom gin, Lemon juice, Lime juice, Sugar syrup (2:1) (+ 5 more) in 8 easy to follow steps

garnish: RIM glass with salt and pepper (moisten outside edge with lime or orange juice and dip into mix of salt and cracked black pepper).

name: Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. Cut a ripe lime in half (from pole to pole) and remove the core from each half by cutting a V-shaped groove. (You'll need one-half of the lime per cocktail.) CHOP into wedges and each wedge chopped in half before dropping the resulting 6 or 8 lime chunks into the base of shaker. Add 3 bar spoons powdered sugar [or 15ml (½oz) sugar syrup 2:1] and MUDDLE. If using cubed ice straight from the freezer, add chilled water (or optionally add fruit of choice) and MUDDLE some more. If using watery ice then omit some or all of the water/juice. When sure the lime's juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, POUR the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the shaker tin used to muddle and swirl to rinse tin before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to combine with other ingredients. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Serve with a stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane) but no straws.
comment: Discover how to make a Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Powdered sugar, Chilled water and Cachaça in 10 easy to follow steps

name: Pineapple and Mint Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add pineapple and mint and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Serve with stirrer.
comment: Discover how to make a Pineapple and Mint Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Pineapple (fresh) (+ 2 more) in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Red Grape Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add grapes and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. Fill glass with ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Serve with a stirrer.
comment: Discover how to make a Red Grape Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Red grapes (seedless) and Cachaça in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Sweet Grape Caipirinha (Caipirinha D'uva)
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add red grapes and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) and Sauternes into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer and serve without a straw.
comment: Discover how to make a Sweet Grape Caipirinha (Caipirinha D'uva) using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar (+ 3 more) in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Morning Glory Fizz
ingredients: Top up with Soda (club soda) water
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. SHAKE first 6 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) to emulsify. STRAIN and slowly pour 2/3rds of the contents of the shaker from a higher than normal height into a chilled empty glass (no ice in glass) while simultaneously pouring soda with your other hand. The cocktail and soda should form a single stream of liquid falling into the glass. As when pouring a Guinness, to achieve a perfect head on a fizz, the cocktail should be left to settle for at least a minute before topping off with what remains in the shaker. Ideally, during the settling period, the cocktail should be placed in a glass froster/fridge/freezer. Slowly POUR the final contents of the shaker into the glass. The head should rise like a soufflé.
garnish: Garnish with lime slice wheel.
comment: Discover how to make a Morning Glory Fizz using Speyside single malt, Absinthe, Lemon juice, Lime juice (+ 3 more) in 8 easy to follow steps

name: Passion Fruit Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Chop passion fruit in half and scoop flesh into the rocks glass and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Passion Fruit Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Passion fruit and Cachaça in 11 easy to follow steps

There have been periods when it has been fashionable to coat the rim of the glass in which this cocktail is served with sugar. The earliest written reference to this is in 1934. Thankfully, sugar rims are now out of vogue, and, as Embury writes in his book, 'A twist of lemon may be used if desired and the peel dropped into the glass. Otherwise no decoration.'.

name: Tom and Jerry (serves 6)
ingredients: 15 ml OP high ester aged pot still rum
instructions: Select and pre-heat a TODDY GLASS. Pre-warm six 20cl (6.75oz) toddy glasses or Tom and Jerry mugs with hot water. Using a whisk, BEAT the yolks in a mixing bowl 'until they are thin as water.' Add sugar, Jamaican rum, spices and STIR thoroughly with whisk. In a separate bowl, use a whisk to BEAT the egg whites 'to a stiff froth.' Use a tablespoon to FOLD the frothy egg whites into the spiced and beaten yolk mixture. This produces the gloopy base mixture known as 'Tom and Jerry batter'. To make individual drinks, to each of the warmed glasses or mugs, POUR cognac and rum, and add two table spoons of Tom and Jerry batter. STIR each glass/mug thoroughly with a barspoon while topping with boiling water.
garnish: Each should end up with frothy head ready to receive the garnish of grated nutmeg.
comment: Discover how to make a Tom and Jerry (serves 6) using Egg white, Egg yolk, Superfine/caster sugar (+ 7 more) in 8 easy to follow steps

name: Tropical Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass) <strong>4</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer), coconut liqueur and pineapple juice (also chilled) into the shaker base or glass used to muddle and swirl to rinse before pouring into serving glass STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>5.</strong> Use an <a href="/bar-equipment-and-barware/250/ice-tapper">Ice Tapper</a> to crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Tropical Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Powdered sugar, Cachaça, Coconut rum liqueur and Pineapple juice with easy to follow steps

name: Brandy Crusta
instructions: Select and pre-chill a FLUTE GLASS. Prepare the glass with citrus peel and sugar CRUSTA by first finding a whole lemon that fits snugly inside the top of your glass with its equator in contact with the rim of the glass. Cut off both ends of the fruit to leave a central slice a little more than 25ml (1 inch) thick. Carefully remove the pulp from this central slice to leave a ring of skin and push this into the top of the glass. Moisten the outside rim of the glass and exposed fruit with citrus juice and dip in white caster sugar to heavily frost the edge of both peel and glass. Leave for a couple of hours to form a hard crust that helps secure the peel. (The ring of citrus peel should become a waterproof extension to the top of the glass.) Place in a freezer to chill the glass ready for the cocktail. SHAKE all ingredients with ice. FINE STRAIN into pre-prepared glass.

name: Blueberry Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add blueberries and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to combine all ingredients. Fill glass with ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws). STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Blueberry Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Blueberries (fresh) and Cachaça in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Strawberry and Basil Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons powdered sugar [or 15ml (½oz) sugar syrup 2:1] and MUDDLE. Add strawberries and basil, and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to combine all ingredients. Fill glass with ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer and serve without a straw.
comment: Discover how to make a Strawberry and Basil Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Powdered sugar, Strawberries (fresh) (+ 2 more) in 10 easy to follow steps

name: Kiwi Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons of caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add kiwi slices and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. Fill glass with ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer (but no straws).

name: Watermelon Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. Cut a ripe lime in half (from pole to pole) and remove the core from each half by cutting a V-shaped groove. (You'll need one-half of the lime per cocktail.) CHOP into wedges and each wedge chopped in half before dropping the resulting 6 or 8 lime chunks into the base of shaker. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add watermelon and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. Fill glass with ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Watermelon Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Powdered sugar, Watermelon (fresh) and Cachaça in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Melon Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> Add melon and MUDDLE some more <strong>4.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>6.</strong> Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an <a href="/bar-equipment-and-barware/250/ice-tapper">Ice Tapper</a> and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Melon Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Melon (Cantaloupe) and Cachaça with easy to follow steps

name: Melon and Apple Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> Add melon and MUDDLE some more <strong>4.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass Add apple juice and STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>6.</strong> Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an <a href="/bar-equipment-and-barware/250/ice-tapper">Ice Tapper</a> and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Melon and Apple Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Melon (Cantaloupe) (+ 2 more) with easy to follow steps

name: Pineapple Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add pineapple and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer and serve without a straw.
comment: Discover how to make a Pineapple Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Pineapple (fresh) and Cachaça in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Clarified Vodka Milk Punch
instructions: <strong>Milk Punch Base pre-batch</strong> (scale-up recipe according to requirement) <strong>1.</strong> POUR first 7 ingredients (with peels from the zests lemons and orange) into a sealable container and leave to infuse for 12 hours in a refrigerator <strong>2. Hot milk method</strong> HEAT milk until just starting to simmer and pour (while still simmering) the vodka tea and juice mix into the milk while stirring As soon as the milk curdles, stop stirring Chill for at least 2-3 hours and up to 24 hours <strong>OR 2. Cold milk method</strong> POUR vodka tea and juice mix into cold milk while stirring As soon as the milk curdles, stop stirring Chill for at least 2-3 hours and up to 24 hours <strong>3.</strong> Fine STRAIN mixture through cheesecloth or Superbag into a large jar or another container Ideally, this should be in a refrigerator (I removed a shelf in ours) with the cheesecloth/bag hung over the jar <strong>4.</strong> Repeat step three, without removing curds from the filter or move to a finer strainer (e . from a 400-micron bag to a 100-micron bag) or coffee filter <strong>5.</strong> Stored in the refrigerator, this will keep for a week (and potentially months), but be sure to sterilise containers, etc <strong>Cocktail itself</strong> STIR 50ml pre-prepared Milk Punch Base with 50ml vodka and 7.5ml sugar syrup and strain into an ice-filled glass (preferably over a chunk of block ice)
comment: Discover how to make a Clarified Vodka Milk Punch using Cold breakfast tea, Lemon juice, Lemon peel, Orange juice (+ 6 more) with easy to follow steps

name: Strawberry Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. CUT lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass. Add 3 bar spoons powdered caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE. Add strawberries and MUDDLE some more. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, TRANSFER the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Garnish with stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Strawberry Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Powdered sugar, Strawberries (fresh) and Cachaça in 11 easy to follow steps

name: Bird of Paradise Gin Fizz
ingredients: Top up with Soda (club soda) water
instructions: SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker DRY SHAKE (without ice) and slowly pour 2/3rds of the contents of the shaker from a higher than normal height into a chilled empty glass (no ice in glass) while simultaneously pouring soda with your other hand The cocktail and soda should form a single stream of liquid falling into the glass As when pouring a Guinness, to achieve a perfect head on a fizz, the cocktail should be left to settle for at least a minute before topping off with what remains in the shaker Ideally, during the settling period, the glass should be placed in a glass froster/fridge/freezer As the final contents of the shaker are poured into the glass so the head should rise like a souffle
comment: Discover how to make a Bird of Paradise Gin Fizz using Old Tom gin, Lime juice, Raspberry syrup, Orange flower water (+ 2 more) with easy to follow steps

name: Dawa
instructions: Select and pre-chill a DOUBLE OLD-FASHIONED GLASS. Cut a ripe lime in half (from pole to pole) and remove the core from each half by cutting a V-shape grove. (You'll need one-half of the lime per cocktail). Chop a cored half into 3 wedges, then slice each wedge in half to produce 6 chunks of lime and drop these into the serving glass. MUDDLE lime chunks in the base of the glass. Add vodka and honey-loaded honey dipper stick (or bar spoon of honey) and use the dipper stick (or spoon) to STIR until the honey is dissolved. Add sugar syrup and fill the glass with crushed ice leaving the dipper stick as a garnish for the drinker to continue stirring.
garnish: Prepare garnish of honey-coated dipper stick stirrer (honey stick).
comment: Discover how to make a Dawa using Lime (fresh), Vodka, Honey and Brown sugar syrup in 7 easy to follow steps

name: Duke's Dry Martini - The 'Naked' or 'Direct' Martini
instructions: Select a MARTINI GLASS. FREEZE glass and bottle of gin by placing them in a freezer for at least two hours before making the cocktail. An hour before making drink POUR water into frozen glass, swirl around to coat and place back in freezer. Preferably do this with a little of the water at a time. Continue to swirl around during freezing process to coat the sides of the glass. When ready to serve, POUR chilled vermouth into icy glass and swirl to coat with vermouth. POUR frozen gin into glass. Serve immediately.
garnish: Garnish with skewered olive or lemon zest twist.
comment: Discover how to make a Duke's Dry Martini - The 'Naked' or 'Direct' Martini using Chilled water, Dry vermouth and Gin in 8 easy to follow steps

name: Earl Grey MarTEAni
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS. INFUSE gin with black tea (preferably Earl Grey). Rather than infuse a whole bottle, you can instead pour just 120ml (4oz) dry gin (enough for two cocktails) into a small glass with one Earl Grey tea bag for 5 minutes at room temperature to achieve the desired flavour extraction. (Any longer and tannins can be overly dominant.) SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) to emulsify. FINE STRAIN into chilled glass. EXPRESS lemon zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lemon zest twist.

name: Raspberry Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> Add raspberries and MUDDLE some more <strong>4.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>6.</strong> Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws)
comment: Discover how to make a Raspberry Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Raspberries and Cachaça with easy to follow steps

name: Ginger and Fig Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> Add fig and MUDDLE some more <strong>4.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) and ginger liqueur into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>6.</strong> Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
comment: Discover how to make a Ginger and Fig Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Figs, Ginger liqueur and Cachaça with easy to follow steps

name: Cucumber Jalapeño Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> Add cucumber and jalapeño and MUDDLE some more <strong>4.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>6.</strong> Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws)
comment: Discover how to make a Cucumber Jalapeño Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Superfine/caster sugar, Cucumber (fresh) (+ 2 more) with easy to follow steps

name: Banana Caipirinha
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> Add slices of banana and MUDDLE some more <strong>4.</strong> When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed <strong>6.</strong> Use an <a href="/bar-equipment-and-barware/250/ice-tapper">Ice Tapper</a> to crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

name: Blackberry Agave Caipirinha
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS. Cut a ripe lime in half (from pole to pole) and remove the core from each half by cutting a V-shaped groove. (You'll need one-half of the lime per cocktail.) CHOP into wedges and each wedge chopped in half before dropping the resulting 6 or 8 lime chunks into the base of shaker. Add agave syrup and MUDDLE. Add blackberries and MUDDLE some more. When sure the lime and blackberry juice is fully extracted, POUR the muddled fruit into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz highball glass). POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the shaker tin used to muddle and swirl to rinse tin before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to combine with other ingredients. FILL glass with small cubes or cracked ice. (If using large ice cubes, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an ICE TAPPER and drop into serving glass.) Briefly STIR before topping with more ice.
garnish: Serve with a stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane) but no straws.
comment: Discover how to make a Blackberry Agave Caipirinha using Lime (fresh), Blackberries, Agave syrup and Cachaça in 10 easy to follow steps

name: Caipirinha (no added sugar and low-calorie)
instructions: <strong>1.</strong> Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and slice on half into 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass <strong>2.</strong> Add sweetener and MUDDLE <strong>3.</strong> If using cubed ice straight from the freezer, add chilled water (or optionally add fruit of your choice) and MUDDLE some more If using watery ice then omit water <strong>4.</strong> When sure muddling has fully extracted fruit juice, POUR the muddled fruit and sweetener into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass) <strong>5.</strong> POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (the glass used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass STIR to mix with other ingredients <strong>6.</strong> If using cubed ice, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws)
comment: Discover how to make a Caipirinha (no added sugar and low-calorie) using Lime (fresh), Sugar-free sweetener syrup (+ 2 more) with easy to follow steps

name: Absinthe Drip Cocktail (French method)
instructions: Select and pre-chill an ABSINTHE GLASS OR OLD-FASHIONED GLASS. POUR absinthe into glass. PLACE cube of sugar on a slotted absinthe spoon resting across the top of the glass. Using an absinthe fountain (or a bottle of chilled mineral water with a small hole in the cap), DRIP iced water over the sugar so it dissolves and drips into the glass. Traditionally the same amount of water is added as absinthe, but full-strength absinthe requires more dilution - Keep dripping until absinthe louches (turns milky white). Add ice, stir and serve.
comment: Discover how to make an Absinthe Drip Cocktail (French method) using Absinthe, Chilled water and Sugar cube in 6 easy to follow steps

instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS. STIR all ingredients with ice. FINE STRAIN into chilled glass. EXPRESS flamed orange zest twist over cocktail, then use as garnish.

name: Honolulu
instructions: Select and pre-chill an OLD-FASHIONED GLASS. DRY BLEND (without ice) first 5 ingredients on slow setting and then gradually add Xanthan gum while continuing to blend. Add 7oz scoop of crushed ice and BLEND some more. POUR into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with pineapple wedge on rim skewered with maraschino cherry.
